key_insight: A neural-network time integrator can replace costly plasticity state updates while preserving the plastic consistency condition and accelerating constitutive calculations.
engineering_relevance: This can reduce the runtime of finite element simulations with nonlinear plasticity, especially when many integration-point updates are required.
method: Train neural networks to predict internal state variable updates for a von Mises plasticity model with nonlinear kinematic hardening.
Use invariant-based inputs and analytically determined evolution directions from the training data.
Enforce exact plastic consistency condition satisfaction during time integration.
evidence_signal: The framework was trained on only 10 cycles of multiaxial proportional loading and then validated in material point and finite element simulations, showing good accuracy, stability, and lower computation time.
watch_item: The approach was demonstrated on a prototype plasticity model and may need retraining or reformulation for more complex materials, load paths, or extrapolated conditions.
paper_title: Machine Learning-Accelerated Time Integration of Plasticity Models
